# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Tests from External Contributors

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
    branches:
      - main
      - develop

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write
  checks: write

jobs:
  security-check:
    name: Security Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      approved: ${{ steps.check.outputs.approved }}
    steps:
      - name: Check if PR is from trusted source
        id: check
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}" == "${{ github.repository }}" ]]; then
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          elif [[ "${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'safe-to-test') }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "approved=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Comment on unapproved PR
        if: steps.check.outputs.approved == 'false'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: '⚠️ This PR requires manual approval before tests can run. A maintainer will review and add the `safe-to-test` label if appropriate.'
            })

  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: security-check
    if: needs.security-check.outputs.approved == 'true'
    
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [16, 18, 20]
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          repository: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Setup Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci --legacy-peer-deps
        env:
          NODE_ENV: test

      - name: Run linting
        id: lint
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          npm run lint > lint-results.txt 2>&1 || true
          echo "lint_exit_code=$?" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Run unit tests
        id: test
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          npm test -- --coverage --ci --json --outputFile=test-results.json > test-output.txt 2>&1 || true
          echo "test_exit_code=$?" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Run build
        id: build
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          npm run build > build-output.txt 2>&1 || true
          echo "build_exit_code=$?" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Parse test results
        id: parse-results
        run: |
          if [ -f test-results.json ]; then
            total_tests=$(jq '.numTotalTests // 0' test-results.json)
            passed_tests=$(jq '.numPassedTests // 0' test-results.json)
            failed_tests=$(jq '.numFailedTests // 0' test-results.json)
            echo "total_tests=$total_tests" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "passed_tests=$passed_tests" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "failed_tests=$failed_tests" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "total_tests=0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "passed_tests=0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "failed_tests=0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Upload test coverage
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-${{ matrix.node-version }}
          path: coverage/
          retention-days: 7

      - name: Upload test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-results-${{ matrix.node-version }}
          path: |
            test-results.json
            test-output.txt
            lint-results.txt
            build-output.txt
          retention-days: 7

      - name: Set job status
        id: status
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ steps.lint.outputs.lint_exit_code }}" != "0" ]] || \
             [[ "${{ steps.test.outputs.test_exit_code }}" != "0" ]] || \
             [[ "${{ steps.build.outputs.build_exit_code }}" != "0" ]]; then
            echo "success=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "success=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

    outputs:
      success: ${{ steps.status.outputs.success }}
      total_tests: ${{ steps.parse-results.outputs.total_tests }}
      passed_tests: ${{ steps.parse-results.outputs.passed_tests }}
      failed_tests: ${{ steps.parse-results.outputs.failed_tests }}
      lint_exit_code: ${{ steps.lint.outputs.lint_exit_code }}
      test_exit_code: ${{ steps.test.outputs.test_exit_code }}
      build_exit_code: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_exit_code }}

  post-results:
    name: Post Test Results
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build-and-test]
    if: always() && needs.build-and-test.result != 'skipped'
    
    steps:
      - name: Download test artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          pattern: test-results-*
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Create test results comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            
            let lintResults = '';
            let testOutput = '';
            let buildOutput = '';
            
            try {
              if (fs.existsSync('lint-results.txt')) {
                lintResults = fs.readFileSync('lint-results.txt', 'utf8').slice(0, 500);
              }
              if (fs.existsSync('test-output.txt')) {
                testOutput = fs.readFileSync('test-output.txt', 'utf8').slice(0, 1000);
              }
              if (fs.existsSync('build-output.txt')) {
                buildOutput = fs.readFileSync('build-output.txt', 'utf8').slice(0, 500);
              }
            } catch (error) {
              console.log('Error reading result files:', error);
            }

            const lintStatus = '${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.lint_exit_code }}' === '0' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const testStatus = '${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.test_exit_code }}' === '0' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const buildStatus = '${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.build_exit_code }}' === '0' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const overallStatus = '${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.success }}' === 'true' ? '✅ All checks passed!' : '❌ Some checks failed';

            const totalTests = '${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.total_tests }}';
            const passedTests = '${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.passed_tests }}';
            const failedTests = '${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.failed_tests }}';

            const comment = `## ${overallStatus}

            ### Test Results Summary
            
            | Check | Status | Details |
            |-------|--------|---------|
            | Linting | ${lintStatus} | Exit code: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.lint_exit_code }} |
            | Tests | ${testStatus} | ${passedTests}/${totalTests} passed, ${failedTests} failed |
            | Build | ${buildStatus} | Exit code: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.build_exit_code }} |

            ### Test Details
            **Total Tests:** ${totalTests}
            **Passed:** ${passedTests}
            **Failed:** ${failedTests}

            ${testOutput ? `<details><summary>Test Output (truncated)</summary>\n\n\`\`\`\n${testOutput}\n\`\`\`\n</details>` : ''}
            ${lintResults ? `<details><summary>Lint Results (truncated)</summary>\n\n\`\`\`\n${lintResults}\n\`\`\`\n</details>` : ''}
            ${buildOutput ? `<details><summary>Build Output (truncated)</summary>\n\n\`\`\`\n${buildOutput}\n\`\`\`\n</details>` : ''}

            ---
            *Workflow run: [#${{ github.run_number }}](${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }})*
            *Commit: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}*
            `;

            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: comment
            });

      - name: Update check status
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        if: always()
        with:
          script: |
            const conclusion = '${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.success }}' === 'true' ? 'success' : 'failure';
            
            await github.rest.checks.create({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              name: 'PR Tests Summary',
              head_sha: '${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}',
              status: 'completed',
              conclusion: conclusion,
              output: {
                title: conclusion === 'success' ? 'All tests passed' : 'Some tests failed',
                summary: `Tests: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.passed_tests }}/${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.total_tests }} passed`,
                text: `Lint: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.lint_exit_code === '0' && 'Passed' || 'Failed' }}\nBuild: ${{ needs.build-and-test.outputs.build_exit_code === '0' && 'Passed' || 'Failed' }}`
              }
            });

  add-labels:
    name: Add Labels
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build-and-test]
    if: always() && needs.build-and-test.result != 'skipped'
    
    steps:
      - name: Add success label
        if: needs.build-and-test.outputs.success == 'true'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            await github.rest.issues.addLabels({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              labels: ['tests-passing']
            });
            
            try {
              await github.rest.issues.removeLabel({
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                name: 'tests-failing'
              });
            } catch (error) {
              console.log('Label tests-failing does not exist');
            }

      - name: Add failure label
        if: needs.build-and-test.outputs.success != 'true'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            await github.rest.issues.addLabels({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              labels: ['tests-failing']
            });
            
            try {
              await github.rest.issues.removeLabel({
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                name: 'tests-passing'
              });
            } catch (error) {
              console.log('Label tests-passing does not exist');
            }
